# Career Atlas 🧭

**An AI-powered career discovery platform for students.** Take a short, adaptive quiz and get ranked career matches, a breakdown of your strengths and gaps, and a concrete roadmap to get there — all generated by an AI in real time.

🔗 **Live demo:** [career-ai-eta-seven.vercel.app](https://career-ai-eta-seven.vercel.app)

> Sign up with any email and take the quiz end to end — the questions and the final report are generated live by an AI model.

---

## ✨ Features

- **Adaptive AI quiz** — the number of questions is dynamic (8–12), sized to the user instead of being fixed. A mix of multiple-choice (with a write-your-own "Other" option) and an open-ended final question.
- **AI career report** — top career matches with fit reasoning, strengths, skill gaps, and an ordered roadmap.
- **Persistent progress** — each user's profile, answers, and results are saved to their own Firestore document.
- **Provider-agnostic AI** — the same app runs on a **local open-source model (Ollama)** during development and a **hosted model (Google Gemini)** in production, switched by a single environment variable.
- **Secured by design** — API endpoint requires an authenticated Firebase user, enforces per-user rate limiting, and the database is locked down with Firestore security rules.
- **Resilient to bad AI output** — model responses are validated with `zod`; malformed output shows a clean "regenerate" state instead of crashing.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture highlight: the switchable AI layer

The app never talks to an AI model directly. Every AI call goes through a single serverless function (`api/chat.js`) that acts as one doorway. That function reads a `MODEL_PROVIDER` environment variable and routes the request accordingly, while always returning the **same response shape** so the frontend never changes:

```
Browser ──▶ /api/chat ──▶  ┌─ MODEL_PROVIDER=ollama  → local Llama 3.1 (dev)
                           └─ MODEL_PROVIDER=gemini  → Google Gemini  (prod)
                                    │
                           returns { content: [{ text }] } either way
```

This means the deployed site uses a reliable hosted model (so it always works for visitors), while local development runs a free open-source model with no API cost — without maintaining two codebases.

---

## 🛠️ Tech stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Frontend | React 18, Vite, React Router v7 |
| Auth & Database | Firebase Authentication, Cloud Firestore |
| AI (production) | Google Gemini (`gemini-2.5-flash`) |
| AI (local dev) | Ollama (`llama3.1:8b`) |
| Validation | Zod |
| Hosting | Vercel (static frontend + serverless function) |

---

## 🚀 Running locally

**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, a Firebase project, and [Vercel CLI](https://vercel.com/docs/cli) (`npm i -g vercel`). For the local AI model, install [Ollama](https://ollama.com).

```bash
# 1. Clone and install
git clone https://github.com/dakshxndrm/career-ai.git
cd career-ai
npm install

# 2. Pull the local model (only needed for the Ollama provider)
ollama pull llama3.1:8b

# 3. Create .env.local (see Environment variables below)

# 4. Run — `vercel dev` serves BOTH the app and the api/ function
vercel dev
```

> Note: use `vercel dev`, not `npm run dev`. The app's AI calls live in `api/chat.js`, a serverless function that plain Vite does not run.

---

## 🔑 Environment variables

Create a `.env.local` file in the project root for local development, and set the same variables in your Vercel project settings for production.

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `MODEL_PROVIDER` | `ollama` for local, `gemini` for production |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Gemini API key — required when provider is `gemini`. Get one free at [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com) |
| `FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY` | Your Firebase web API key, used by the backend to verify auth tokens |

**Example `.env.local` (local dev with Ollama):**
```env
MODEL_PROVIDER=ollama
FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY=your_firebase_web_api_key
```

**Production (Vercel) values:**
```env
MODEL_PROVIDER=gemini
GEMINI_API_KEY=your_gemini_api_key
FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY=your_firebase_web_api_key
```

> Never commit real keys. The `GEMINI_API_KEY` is a secret. (A Firebase *web* API key is safe to expose — it identifies the project but grants no access on its own; data is protected by Firestore security rules and Auth.)

---

## 🔒 Security

- **API access** — `api/chat.js` rejects any request without a valid Firebase ID token, so the AI endpoint can't be called anonymously.
- **Rate limiting** — each user is capped at a fixed number of AI requests per hour.
- **Database rules** — `firestore.rules` restricts every user to reading and writing only their own documents; all other access is denied.

---

## 📁 Project structure

```
career-ai/
├── api/
│   └── chat.js              # Serverless AI doorway (provider-agnostic)
├── firestore.rules          # Database access rules
├── src/
│   ├── firebase.js          # Firebase initialization
│   ├── auth.js              # Sign-up / sign-in helpers
│   ├── App.jsx              # Routes
│   ├── context/
│   │   └── AuthContext.jsx  # App-wide auth state
│   ├── components/          # Navbar, ProtectedRoute
│   └── pages/
│       ├── DashboardPublic.jsx  # Landing page
│       ├── Auth.jsx             # Login / signup
│       ├── Profile.jsx          # User profile
│       ├── Assessment.jsx       # The AI quiz
│       └── Results.jsx          # The AI career report
└── README.md
```

---

## 🗺️ Roadmap / possible next steps

- [ ] Migrate inline styles to a design-token system (Tailwind + shadcn/ui)
- [ ] Add distributed rate limiting (e.g. Upstash) for multi-instance scaling
- [ ] Let users revisit and compare past assessment results
- [ ] Export the career report as a shareable PDF

---

## 📝 Design

The interface uses a custom **"Career Atlas"** identity — an atlas / field-guide feel built around the idea of charting your course. Typography pairs **Fraunces** (display serif) with **Inter** (UI), on an ink / marigold / sage palette with a topographic contour-line motif.
